# Test: Cross-Repository Dependency Chain (US2)
# Validates that schema dependencies work across multiple application repositories.
# App2 (user service) consumes App1's (payment service) released schema and releases its own.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Initialize canonical repository with common schemas
mkdir canonical-repo
cd canonical-repo
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init canonical --org=testorg --repo=cross-deps-canonical --skip-git --non-interactive
exists buf.yaml
exists catalog/catalog.yaml

# Create common schema
exec mkdir -p proto/common/v1
cp ../common.proto proto/common/v1/common.proto

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Initial canonical schemas'
exec git tag v1.0.0
cd ..

# Step 2: Initialize app1 (payment service) - the "producer"
mkdir app1-payment
cd app1-payment
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --repo=test-app --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/payments/ledger/v1
exists apx.yaml
exists internal/apis/proto/payments/ledger/v1/ledger.proto

# Add dependency on canonical common schemas
exec apx add proto/common/v1@v1.0.0
exists apx.lock
exec grep 'proto/common/v1' apx.lock
exec grep 'v1.0.0' apx.lock

# Verify apx.yaml has dependency listed
exec grep 'proto/common/v1' apx.yaml

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Payment service with common dependency'
exec git tag proto/payments/ledger/v1/v1.0.0
cd ..

# Step 3: Initialize app2 (user service) - the "consumer"
mkdir app2-user
cd app2-user
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'E2E Test Bot'
exec git config user.email 'e2e@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --repo=test-app --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/users/profile/v1
exists apx.yaml
exists internal/apis/proto/users/profile/v1/profile.proto

# App2 depends on same canonical repo
exec apx add proto/common/v1@v1.0.0
exists apx.lock
exec grep 'proto/common/v1' apx.lock

# App2 also depends on app1's payment API
exec apx add proto/payments/ledger/v1@v1.0.0
exec grep 'proto/payments/ledger/v1' apx.yaml
exec grep 'proto/payments/ledger/v1' apx.lock

exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'User service with payment and common dependencies'
exec git tag proto/users/profile/v1/v1.0.0
cd ..

# Step 4: Verify both apps can independently release (dry-run)
cd app1-payment
exec apx release prepare proto/payments/ledger/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/cross-deps-canonical --version=v1.0.0 --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'
cd ..

cd app2-user
exec apx release prepare proto/users/profile/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/cross-deps-canonical --version=v1.0.0 --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'
cd ..

# Step 5: Validate dependency chain is consistent
cd app2-user
exec grep 'proto/common/v1' apx.lock
exec grep 'proto/payments/ledger/v1' apx.lock
cd ..

# Step 6: Verify git history in both apps
cd app1-payment
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Payment service'
cd ..

cd app2-user
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'User service'
cd ..

# Success: Cross-repository dependency chain validated
# - App1 depends on canonical common schemas
# - App2 depends on both canonical and app1 schemas
# - Both apps can release independently
# - Dependency versions are locked correctly

-- common.proto --
syntax = "proto3";

package common.v1;

option go_package = "github.com/testorg/cross-deps-canonical/gen/go/common/v1;commonv1";

message Money {
  string currency = 1;
  int64 amount = 2;
}

message Address {
  string street = 1;
  string city = 2;
  string state = 3;
  string postal_code = 4;
  string country = 5;
}
